title: Method and mechanism for imprinting shaped images onto a surface
abstract: The present invention relates to a device and method that allows a user to transfer a flowable viscous material onto a surface from a squeezable bottle in a specific desired shape. In the preferred embodiment of the invention, the invention consists of an assemblage of elements constructed to allow a viscous material to be squeezed from a flexible, squeezable bottle containing a flowable viscous material through a series of channels to facilitate the easy application of a decorative shape(s) onto a surface.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A device for transferring a flowable viscous material onto a surface wherein the device comprises: a stamper; and a squeezable bottle comprising: a tip; and a flowable viscous material; wherein the stamper comprises: a rigid piece; and a flexible piece that are removably coupled together; wherein the rigid piece fits within the flexible piece such they are sealed together around their respective edges and create a space between the two pieces; wherein the flexible piece comprises: a first side with a first set of channels; a second side with a second set of channels defining a stencil shape design; and a set of holes connecting the channels on the first side to the channels on the second side; wherein the rigid piece comprises: a first side with an attachment piece that rises above the surface of the first side and removably couples to the tip of the squeezable bottle; and a second side that is smooth and contacts the first side of the flexible piece; wherein when the second side of the rigid piece and the first side of the flexible piece come in contact the space forms a channel pathway for the flowable viscous material to move through when the squeezable bottle is coupled to the attachment piece of the rigid piece and the flowable viscous material moves into the channels on the first side of the flexible piece through entrance holes to the second side of the flexible piece and to the exit holes on the second side of the flexible piece such that the flowable viscous material moves through pathways in the stencil shape and through exit holes and is transferred onto the surface in the desired shape. 2. The device of claim 1 wherein there is a space between the channels on the first side of the flexible piece and the channels on the second side of the flexible piece such that the flowable viscous material in the first level channels does not touch the surface. 3. The device of claim 2 wherein the flowable viscous material in the second level that moves through the holes and into the stencil is transferred to the surface in the desired stencil shape. 4. The device of claim 1 where in the first side of the flexible piece further comprises a rim that comprises vent openings wherein extra flowable viscous material and air escapes the channels and collect within the stamper and is not transferred to the surface. 5. The device of claim 1 wherein the flexible piece and rigid piece are a circular shape. 6. The device of claim 1 wherein the flexible piece and rigid piece are an elliptical shape. 7. The device of claim 1 wherein the flexible piece and rigid piece are a triangular shape. 8. The device of claim 1 wherein the pathways in the stencil shape comprise gaps to allow air in to the system preventing a vacuum and splattering of the viscous material on the surface. 9. The device of claim 1 wherein the flexible piece of the stamper includes a ridge around the stencil shape that prevents the viscous material from running on the surface. 10. The device of claim 1 wherein the stencil shape is a shape selected from the group consisting of a star, a heart, a butterfly, a flower. 11. The device of claim 1 wherein a hole is included through the flexible piece central to the stencil shape to provide a path for air to be drawn into the flexible piece without sucking the viscous material from the stencil when squeezing is relaxed. 12. The device of claim 1 wherein the viscous material is a material selected from the group consisting of gel, glitter glue, or paint.
